introSection: Twister Coaster (天旋地转) was a steel spinning roller coaster located at Chimelong Paradise in Panyu, Guangzhou, Guangdong, China.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Twister Coaster opened with the park in February 2006. It was last seen operating on a visit in September 2009. Photographs taken on 28 February 2011 showed the ride partially dismantled and in July 2011 the ride was seen dismantled and in storage. It has since been removed.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: When it first opened, the ride was painted yellow with green supports. At some point before 2009, it was repainted red with purple supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Twister Coaster has spinning cars which seat riders back-to-back in two rows of two.

sections.content: The following photographs were taken of the ride while it was being dismantled.

fullText:
Twister Coaster (天旋地转) was a steel spinning roller coaster located at Chimelong Paradise in Panyu, Guangzhou, Guangdong, China.

Twister Coaster opened with the park in February 2006. It was last seen operating on a visit in September 2009. Photographs taken on 28 February 2011 showed the ride partially dismantled and in July 2011 the ride was seen dismantled and in storage. It has since been removed.

When it first opened, the ride was painted yellow with green supports. At some point before 2009, it was repainted red with purple supports.

Twister Coaster has spinning cars which seat riders back-to-back in two rows of two.

The following photographs were taken of the ride while it was being dismantled.
